summary: Closed but unmerged PR that reworked src/melee/lb/lbheap.c to improve lbheap matching, especially lbHeap_80015900, while preserving lbHeap_80015F3C as matched. The PR body says lbHeap_80015900 improved from 94.3% to 96.5%; the decomp.dev bot reported lbHeap_80015900 improved from 81.08% to 96.48% (+107 bytes) and lbHeap_80015F3C became 100.00% (+21 bytes). The patch fixed a documented heap bounds scan issue so heaps 2-5 are covered, introduced local heap helper routines, and used pointer/offset-based views to improve code generation.

key_files.role: Only changed file; introduced lbHeap_HeapDesc, lbHeap_HeapOffsetView, local inline heap reset/create/destroy helpers, and rewrote lbHeap_80015900/lbHeap_80015F3C matching structure.

what_changed: Named the previously anonymous heap descriptor struct as struct lbHeap_HeapDesc and made lbHeap_803BA380 an array of that type.
Added struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView with a 0x10 byte pad followed by struct Heap, plus lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset) to address heap data through an offset from lbHeap_80431FA0.
Factored repeated heap initialization into static inline lbHeap_ResetHeap(struct Heap* heap).

Factored repeated transient heap teardown into static inline lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated(struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView* view), preserving the OSDestroyHeap path for type 0 heaps and lbMemory_80014EEC path for non-type-0 heaps.

Factored repeated heap creation into static inline lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed(struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView* view), preserving OSCreateHeap for type 0 and lbMemory_80014E24 for other heap types.
Reworked lbHeap_80015900 variable types from pointer-like arena/ARAM bounds to u32 arithmetic, with casts only at calls such as HSD_CreateMainHeap and lbMemory_800154D4.

Replaced the old bounds scan, which had a TODO noting it checked heap 2 twice, with a two-iteration loop using bounds_heap = &heap_array[bounds_i * 2 - 2] and bounds_heap++ to inspect heap slots 2, 3, 4, and 5.
Reworked creation/destruction loops in lbHeap_80015900 to track heap_offset starting at 0x38 and increment by sizeof(struct Heap), then access an lbHeap_HeapOffsetView for helper calls.
Changed lbHeap_80015900 to use explicit main_heap and aram_heap local pointers for heap_array[0] and heap_array[1].
Changed lbHeap_80015F3C to call lbHeap_ResetHeap six times explicitly instead of using a for loop over heap_array[0..5].
Changed lbHeap_80015F3C descriptor iteration from an integer index into lbHeap_803BA380 to a struct lbHeap_HeapDesc* pointer that increments until desc->idx == 6.
smart_moves: The patch improved matching by reshaping source control flow to look more like compiler output rather than only simplifying C: explicit unrolled reset calls, pointer-walking descriptor iteration, and offset-based heap views are all likely codegen-driven choices.

The lbHeap_HeapOffsetView wrapper is a targeted aliasing/layout tactic: it lets helper functions operate on a struct containing the heap at the expected offset without changing the underlying global layout exposed elsewhere.
Factoring destroy/create/status logic into static inline helpers kept repeated semantics centralized while still allowing inlining for matching-sensitive code.
The bounds scan rewrite simultaneously addressed a real correctness concern documented in the old TODO and improved matching coverage by scanning heap slots 2-5.
Using u32 for arena_lo, arena_hi, aram_lo, and aram_hi made arithmetic and comparisons explicit and avoided pointer arithmetic artifacts visible in the old code.
decomp_lessons: For heap/memory manager functions, matching may depend on addressing a global struct through byte offsets rather than direct array indexing; introducing a small local view struct can be a valid tactic when backed by observed layout.

Do not assume a loop is semantically correct just because it roughly matches behavior: the old lbHeap_80015900 comment said the bounds loop checked heap 2 twice, and the PR changed it to cover heaps 2-5.

When a reset loop fails to match, try explicit repeated calls to a tiny inline helper; compilers may have unrolled or emitted repeated stores in a way that is easier to reproduce with explicit source.
Pointer iteration over descriptor tables can match better than integer indexing, especially when the loop sentinel is a field value such as idx == 6.
For decomp PRs, include both build verification and checkdiff for the functions being touched; this PR listed ninja plus checkdiff for lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C.
Be cautious interpreting match percentages: the PR body and decomp.dev bot reported different before values for lbHeap_80015900, though both agree the final result was about 96.5%.
naming_conventions: New local/internal helper names preserve the project’s symbol prefix and Pascal-ish function style: lbHeap_ResetHeap, lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated, lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed.
Struct names use the lbHeap_ prefix and descriptive suffixes: struct lbHeap_HeapDesc and struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView.
Local variable names were made role-specific and matching-aware: destroy_view, bounds_heap, create_view, destroy_i, bounds_i, create_i, heap_offset, main_heap, aram_heap, curr_heap, prev_heap, desc.
Existing unknown/global symbol names were retained rather than guessed: lbHeap_803BA380, lbHeap_80431FA0, lbHeap_80015900, lbHeap_80015F3C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a macro for offset-based global access: lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset) casts ((u32)(&lbHeap_80431FA0) + offset) to struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView*.
Track heap_offset starting at 0x38 and increment it by sizeof(struct Heap) in loops over heap slots 2-5.
Use static inline helpers to preserve repeated operations while allowing the compiler to inline them into the caller.
Use pointer increments for table iteration: desc = lbHeap_803BA380; while ((curr_idx = desc->idx) != 6) { ...; desc++; }.
Use direct struct Heap* locals for heap_array entries to influence load/store ordering and reduce repeated global indexing.
Represent arena and ARAM bounds as u32 values during calculation, then cast to void* only at API boundaries.
Manually unroll heap reset calls in lbHeap_80015F3C instead of relying on a for loop.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 reported matched code increased by +0.01% / +544 bytes.
Automated report listed one new match: main/melee/lb/lbheap lbHeap_80015F3C, +21 bytes, 96.29% to 100.00%.
Automated report listed one unmatched-item improvement: main/melee/lb/lbheap lbHeap_80015900, +107 bytes, 81.08% to 96.48%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that superseded or merged lbheap matching improvements after closed unmerged PR #2497.
Search for uses of lbHeap_HeapOffsetView or offset-based views around lbHeap_80431FA0 in later commits.
Compare current lbHeap_80015900 against this PR to see whether the heaps 2-5 bounds scan tactic was reused.
Investigate why the PR body reported lbHeap_80015900 before 94.3% while the decomp.dev bot reported 81.08%.
